nodes.label: good-claude
nodes.summary: This project appears to be a **modular framework** (likely an extension named *Good Claude*) designed to manage various functional components. It utilizes a **Feature Configuration Stub** to establish a standard interface for these components, ensuring they act as **placeholders** (hidden and disabled) by default until they are fully implemented and activated.

children.label: Feature Configuration Stub
children.summary:
This abstraction represents the basic interface for a feature, module, or plugin within the system. It defines core properties required for the system to recognize a component: its identity (`name`), its visibility in the UI (`isHidden`), and its logic for whether it is active (`isEnabled`).

Think of it as a "Coming Soon" storefront in a shopping mall. The framework exists (it has a location and a name tag), but the store is currently closed (disabled) and covered up (hidden) so customers cannot enter or see inside yet. It serves as a placeholder or a default state.
